name: space-ideas
description: One awkward/empty space, N ideas for it. Same architecture in every frame (gen-images --base-first), stop-motion furnishing transitions, English hook.
image_style_preamble: High-quality photorealistic interior photo, vertical 9:16, shot on a phone, natural warm lighting, realistic materials, sharp and well-lit like a real-estate listing. The architecture, walls, floor, lighting and camera angle must stay EXACTLY identical to the base image; only the contents of the space change.
base_space_guidance: The base (frame 1) MUST be a genuinely AWKWARD / UNUSUAL architectural spot that makes 'I didn't know what to do with this space' believable: an under-stairs nook, a recessed wall niche/alcove, the dead space beside a chimney breast, a sloped attic corner, a bay-window nook, an awkward landing, a deep window reveal, a boxed-in bulkhead. NEVER a plain empty room corner (bare wall+window+floor is boring and off-brief). Keep it empty so the ideas fill it.
script_structure: Hook in first 3s: 'I didn't know what to do with this space...'. Then one short line per idea frame naming the idea. End with an engagement question like 'Which one would you pick?'. ~2.2 words/sec.
transition_prompt_template: Playful stop-motion style timelapse in one single continuous shot: {WHAT_CHANGES}, piece by piece. Dynamic but seamless, evenly paced across the whole clip, ending exactly at the final frame. No scene cuts, no flashes. Camera perfectly static on a tripod, identical framing throughout. Constant natural lighting. No people.
telegram_caption: Space transformation ideas 👆 Which one would you pick?
